summary: Energetic performance with standout Sugaree and a scorching Scarlet>Fire solo, but MP3 gaps make the listening experience uneven.
review: Reviewers agree the concert is solid—highlighting Garcia’s fiery solo on Scarlet > Fire and memorable renditions of The Sugaree and Ramble on Rose—but many note that the MP3 files suffer from noticeable gaps between songs, suggesting the loss‑less FLAC version is preferable. Overall the show is enjoyable though not exceptional.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers describe a good but unremarkable performance (average ~3.5/5) while multiple comments criticize the MP3 gaps, lowering recording quality to fair (~2.5/5). Weighted combination yields roughly 3.2 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers mention audible gaps between songs in the MP3 version; lossless FLAC files are available and recommended for a smoother experience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic and well‑played, but the set does not flow as effortlessly as other shows; occasional struggling noted by listeners.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers a scorching solo during Scarlet>Fire that reviewers single out as a highlight.
